| Most common I (M) | 0.1: 2457; 0.5: 498; 1.0: 338; 0.15: 294; 0.0: 244 | Majority near I ≈ 0.1 M |

paragraphs.text:
Notes and context
- Media/conditions: Nearly all Zn(II) stability data here are in water and cluster at 25 °C and I ≈ 0.1 M, with substantial coverage up to I = 1–3 M for anion complexes.
- Ligand families: Alongside simple inorganic anions, SRD‑46 contains many amino acids (glycine, histidine, cysteine), N‑donors (ethylenediamine, imidazole, ammonia), aromatic chelators (1,10‑phenanthroline), and phosphonates (e.g., NTP). EDTA and related aminopolycarboxylates are present; representative entries include hydrolysis‑coupled reactions, and protonation ladders for EDTA derivatives are available at I = 0.1 M.
- Trends: Chloride complexation is weak and increases with ionic strength, while bisulfate is substantially stronger at low I and weakens with I. Bicarbonate shows appreciable cumulative stability, especially for ML2 at higher ionic strength. Among bioligands, thio‑donor substitution (thiohistidine) enhances Zn(II) binding relative to histidine.

claims.text: Nearly all Zn(II) stability data here are in water
EDTA and related aminopolycarboxylates are present
protonation ladders for EDTA derivatives are available
bisulfate is substantially stronger at low I
especially for ML2 at higher ionic strength
thio‑donor substitution (thiohistidine) enhances Zn(II) binding relative to histidine

Zn–HSO4−: logK 2.34 at I=0 vs 0.93 at I=0.5 (25 °C) → stronger at low I
Zn–HCO3−: ML logK 3.9 (I=0.1); ML2 logβ2 5.3 (0.7) and 6.9 (3.0) → appreciable cumulative stability
Zn–HCO3− ML2 (β2): 5.3 at I=0.7 → 6.9 at I=3.0 (25 °C) ⇒ stronger at higher I
Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.
Thiohistidine: logK 9.16 (I=0.1); Histidine: logK 6.51 (I=0.1) → thio enhances binding

paragraphs.text: If you’d like, I can drill into a specific system (e.g., Zn–EDTA at 25 °C, I = 0.1 M; Zn–imidazole; hydrolysis/hydroxo species) and pull full stoichiometries, logβ/stepwise logK, and citations.

tool_snippets.snippet:
## execute_srd46_sql - 15 row(s)
SELECT c.ligand_id, c.ligand_name_SRD, COUNT(s.stability_id) AS n_measurements
FROM ligandmetal_card c
JOIN ligandmetal_stability_measured s ...

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Tool calls use the SRD‑46 driver ('execute_srd46_sql'); the raw outputs do not print 'SRD‑46' verbatim, so provenance is inferred from the tool name.

tool_snippets.snippet:
## execute_srd46_sql - 15 row(s)
SELECT c.ligand_id, c.ligand_name_SRD, COUNT(s.stability_id) AS n_measurements
FROM ligandmetal_card c
JOIN ligandmetal_stability_measured s ON s.card_id = c.card_id

## execute_srd46_sql - 1 row(s)
SELECT s.solvent_name, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM ligandmetal_stability_measured s
JOIN ligandmetal_card c ON c.card_id = s.card_id
WHERE c.metal_id = 208
